mapping_metadata.analyzer: Senior Test Automation Mapping Specialist Agent

test_case_mappings.test_case_description: Test case to verify the Billing Cycle Period card behavior on Print and Payment History page when a plan is paused, including verification of complimentary pages, additional pages, progress bars, tooltips, and page count displays.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Enroll the Ink printer with 50 pages plan or higher.
precondition_mapping.notes: This precondition requires printer enrollment with a specific plan. No printer enrollment methods, API helpers for enrollment, or enrollment page objects were found in this batch. The batch contains helpers for Resque jobs, shipment tracking, and plan updates, but not printer enrollment. Enrollment methods likely exist in other batches containing enrollment page objects or API helper modules.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the Subscription has no free months info.
precondition_mapping.notes: This precondition requires verifying subscription state (no free months). No API helpers for retrieving subscription details or methods for checking free months status were found in this batch. UpdatePlanHelper.doesnt_see_free_months exists but it verifies UI display of free trial info, not the actual subscription state. Subscription state verification methods likely exist in other batches containing subscription API helpers or subscription management modules.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the subscription is "subscribed" status.
precondition_mapping.notes: This precondition requires verifying subscription status is 'subscribed'. No API helpers for retrieving subscription status or methods for verifying subscription state were found in this batch. Subscription status verification methods likely exist in other batches containing subscription API helpers or state management modules.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Click the Pause Plan button on CIT modal or "Pause Instant Ink" link to trigger the pause plan.

components_found_in_this_batch.what_it_does: Navigates to a specific page by clicking on a navigation link with the specified page_name text. Uses an XPath selector to locate an anchor element with class 'nav-link' whose normalized text content exactly matches the provided page_name. This is a general navigation utility that could potentially be used to navigate to the dashboard or page where pause plan action is available.
components_found_in_this_batch.relevance_reason: This precondition requires navigating to a page and clicking a pause plan button or link. While this method provides general page navigation capability, it is designed for clicking nav-link elements in the main navigation menu, not for clicking pause plan buttons within page content. It could be used to navigate to the dashboard where the pause plan action might be initiated, but it does not directly fulfill the pause plan clicking requirement. The actual pause plan button/link clicking would require different methods or page objects not found in this batch.

precondition_mapping.notes: This precondition requires clicking a Pause Plan button on a CIT modal or a 'Pause Instant Ink' link. While RABaseHelper.access_page provides general navigation capability, no specific methods for clicking pause plan buttons, interacting with CIT modals, or triggering plan pause were found in this batch. UpdatePlanHelper.cancellation_subscription exists but it handles subscription cancellation, not plan pause. The DashboardPage page object with pause plan button locators and click methods likely exists in other batches containing dashboard page objects.

matched_page_objects.class_purpose: Base class for all page objects in the framework, providing core functionality for element locator management and dynamic attribute access. This is the foundational class that PrintAndPaymentHistoryPage and DashboardPage (needed for this test) would inherit from. Provides __getattr__ for dynamic locator to Playwright locator conversion, WaitProxy for wait operations, and get_selector for retrieving raw selector strings.
matched_page_objects.how_to_instantiate: BasePageObject is not instantiated directly. Page object classes inherit from it: class PrintAndPaymentHistoryPage(BasePageObject): def __init__(self, page): super().__init__(page)
matched_page_objects.relevance_reason: This test case requires page objects for PrintAndPaymentHistoryPage and DashboardPage to interact with UI elements like billing cycle card, progress bars, tooltips, info icons, and page count displays. BasePageObject is the foundational class that these page objects would inherit from to gain dynamic locator access, wait capabilities, and element interaction infrastructure. While the specific page objects are not in this batch, BasePageObject is relevant as the architectural foundation that enables page object functionality throughout the framework.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Navigates to a specific page by clicking on a navigation link with the specified page_name text. Uses an XPath selector to locate an anchor element with class 'nav-link' whose normalized text content exactly matches the provided page_name.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Steps 1 and 4 require navigating to the Print and Payment History page. This method provides general navigation capability by clicking navigation links. However, it is designed for main navigation menu links (nav-link class), and the actual navigation to Print and Payment History may use different UI patterns. The method could potentially be used if Print and Payment History is accessible via a nav-link in the main menu, but the test case suggests it may be under HP Instant Ink section which might require different navigation approach.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Waits for a specific page to fully load by checking for the presence of an h1 heading element containing the text 'Details for {page_title}'. Uses Playwright's wait_for_selector with a dynamic selector pattern. Default timeout is 60 seconds.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Steps 1 and 4 involve navigating to Print and Payment History page, and the expected results include verifying the page loaded successfully. This method waits for page load by checking for an h1 heading with pattern 'Details for {page_title}'. However, the Print and Payment History page may not follow this exact heading pattern - it might have a different h1 structure. The method is relevant as a wait utility but may need adaptation for the actual page heading format.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Locates and returns a card body element by searching for a card with a specific title in its header. Constructs a CSS selector that matches div.card elements containing h5.card-header with exact text matching the title. Returns a locator for the card body element. Waits up to 10 seconds if card not immediately found.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Steps 2 and 5 require checking the Billing Cycle Period card. This method locates card elements by their title in the card header, which directly matches the test requirement to locate the 'Billing Cycle Period' card. The method searches for div.card with h5.card-header containing the title text, which is a common UI pattern. This is highly relevant for locating the billing cycle card before verifying its contents.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Retrieves the text content from a field within a card section identified by its title. First calls get_item_by_title to locate the card body, then extracts all visible text content using inner_text() and strips whitespace. Returns the cleaned text string.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Multiple steps require reading text content from the Billing Cycle Period card and other sections (complimentary pages value, info messages, total pages printed). This method extracts text content from card sections identified by title, which is useful for reading the displayed information. However, it returns all text from the card body, so additional parsing would be needed to extract specific values like '0 of 10 used' or progress bar percentages.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Verifies that free trial information is not displayed on the update plan page. Uses Playwright's expect() assertion to verify that free_trial_info_card and free_trial_plan_details elements are not visible. This is used in test scenarios where the user should not see free trial offers.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Precondition 2 requires verifying that the subscription has no free months info. This method verifies that free trial information is not displayed on the UI, which is related but not exactly the same as verifying the subscription state has no free months. The method checks UI visibility of free trial elements on the update plan page, whereas the precondition requires backend/API verification of subscription state. The method is tangentially relevant but does not directly fulfill the precondition requirement.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Intercepts attribute access on page objects to dynamically convert locator names into Playwright locator objects. When code accesses self.login_button, this method retrieves the selector string from self.elements and returns page.locator(selector_string). This enables clean syntax like self.login_button.click() instead of self.page.locator(self.elements.login_button).click().
matched_methods.usage_example: # In page object: self.billing_cycle_card.click() - automatically converts to locator
matched_methods.relevance_reason: All test steps that interact with UI elements (billing cycle card, progress bars, tooltips, info icons, value displays) would use page objects that inherit from BasePageObject. This __getattr__ method is the core mechanism that enables page objects to access elements using clean attribute syntax. While not directly called in test code, it is fundamental infrastructure that makes all page object element interactions possible. Every element access in the test would rely on this dynamic locator conversion.

matched_helpers.what_it_does: Provides comprehensive set of static utility methods for Resque job management, page navigation, and data extraction from card-based UI layouts. Contains methods for accessing Resque admin, running scheduled jobs, waiting for job completion, navigating to pages and menus, extracting field data by title, accessing links, validating boolean values, and validating template data with date comparisons.
matched_helpers.relevance_reason: This helper provides utilities for page navigation (access_page) and data extraction from card-based UI layouts (get_item_by_title, get_field_text_by_title). The test case involves navigating to Print and Payment History page and extracting information from the Billing Cycle Period card, which aligns with this helper's capabilities. The card-based data extraction methods are particularly relevant for locating and reading content from the billing cycle card. However, the helper does not provide methods for the specific UI interactions needed in this test (progress bar verification, tooltip interactions, color validation, page count tracking).

test_case_mappings.overall_assessment: This test case requires extensive UI interaction with the Print and Payment History page including billing cycle card verification, progress bar validation, tooltip interactions, and page count tracking, plus API operations for time manipulation and page printing. Batch 10 contains limited relevant components: (1) RABaseHelper provides general page navigation and card-based data extraction utilities that are partially useful for locating the billing cycle card and reading its content, but lacks specific methods for progress bar verification, tooltip interactions, color validation, and page count tracking. (2) BasePageObject provides the foundational page object architecture that the required PrintAndPaymentHistoryPage would inherit from, but the actual page object with specific locators and methods is not in this batch. (3) UpdatePlanHelper and ShipmentTrackingHelper are not relevant to this test case as they handle different functional areas (plan updates and shipment tracking vs. billing cycle and page usage verification). (4) No API helpers for time manipulation, billing charge triggering, or page printing simulation were found. The majority of components needed for this test case - PrintAndPaymentHistoryPage page object with all locators, API helpers for backend operations, progress bar verification methods, tooltip interaction methods, color validation utilities, and page count tracking methods - likely exist in other batches containing page objects for the Print and Payment History page, API helper modules, and UI verification utilities.
